items.title: Counterhacker exposes DPRK unit that made $1M a month working IT jobs
items.summary: The North Korean IT workers coordinated crypto payments through a server using a shared, easy-to-guess password “123456.”

items.title: Bitcoin tops $72K after $280M liquidation targets bears: Will the ‘fragile truce’ hold?
items.summary: Bitcoin and global markets rallied after the US and Iran announced a ceasefire, but data show BTC bears have not closed most of their positions yet.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Bitcoin Depot ATM Operator Says $3.6 Million in BTC Stolen in Corporate Hack
items.summary: The Bitcoin ATM operator disclosed a hack two weeks after attackers gained control of settlement account credentials and stole BTC.

items.title: Researchers Propose New Way to Manage Financial Risk When AI Agents Fumble Trades
items.summary: A newly proposed agentic settlement standard would hold fees in escrow and bring underwriters into AI agent transactions.

items.title: US SEC taps new enforcement chief amid questions over predecessor's exit
items.summary: David Woodcock steps into the role as US senators await answers to questions on the agency’s dropping lawsuits against Justin Sun and several crypto companies.

items.title: Morgan Stanley’s bitcoin ETF draws $34 million on day one
items.summary: Morgan Stanley’s low-fee bitcoin ETF debuted with strong early trading, signaling demand as competition shifts to cost and distribution.

items.title: Bitcoin Miner Cango Sells $143 Million in BTC, Slashes Production Costs
items.summary: NYSE-listed miner Cango reduced costs by 19% by shutting down inefficient equipment, and sold Bitcoin to pay down debt.

items.title: Meta Launches Muse Spark, Its Most Capable AI Yet—But Gemini 3.1 Pro Still Leads the Pack
items.summary: Meta's first model from its Superintelligence team is natively multimodal, built for health reasoning, and genuinely competitive—but it doesn't top every leaderboard.

items.title: Treasury Outlines How Stablecoin Rules Will Fight Illicit Finance Under GENIUS Act
items.summary: The proposed rule prohibits people with criminal backgrounds from serving as the head of stablecoin issuers’ compliance programs.

items.title: Anthropic's Mythos Safety Report Shows It Can No Longer Fully Measure What It Built
items.summary: Anthropic’s Claude Mythos is powerful, but Its own safety report reveals a deeper crisis that's gone largely unnoticed.

items.title: Bitcoin has 3–5 years to prepare for quantum risk, says Bernstein
items.summary: Bitcoin faces a future quantum threat, but Bernstein analysts say risks are concentrated in older wallets and exposed keys, and unlikely to cause existential disruption.

items.title: US Treasury moves forward with GENIUS Act, focusing on illicit finance
items.summary: The proposed rule would direct payment stablecoin issuers to establish AML/CFT and sanctions compliance programs, and be able to “block, freeze, and reject” certain transactions.

items.title: Stablecoin Trading Volume Could Skyrocket to $1.5 Quadrillion by 2035: Chainalysis
items.summary: Generational wealth transfer and point-of-sale adoption could drive stablecoin volumes to eclipse traditional payment rails, says Chainalysis.
